rulings.Case Title: CHATEAU DE LOUIS,LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. GENERAL WINDOW CORPORATION DBA INTERNATIONAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Chateau De Louis, Llc, A California Limited Liability Company, No.Nine, Llc, A California Limited Liability Company, And Real Enterprises, Llc'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or, In The Alternative, That The Matters Involved In This Discovery Be Deemed Admitted, Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2000
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 10, 2013, Line 4. Plaintiffs Chateau De Louis, LLC, No.Nine, LLC, and Real Enterprises, LLC'S Motion for Terminating Sanctions or, in the Alternative, that the Matters Involved in this Discovery Be Deemed Admitted, Request for Monetary Sanctions in the Amount Of $2000 Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs' Motion for Terminating Sanctions, Etc. is DENIED. The Court does not find a misuse of the discovery process under C.C.P. § 2023.010. The Court does not find that Plaintiffs properly noticed a motion to have discovery deemed admitted. Plaintiffs' Request for Sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: COLONIAL MEDICAL GROUP, INC. A CALIFORNIA VS. CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 10, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF COLONIAL MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'s DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the First Amended Answer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the unclean hands and estoppel defenses. Defendant should plead ultimate facts to support those defenses and clarify the basis for the defenses. The demurrer is overruled as to the collateral estoppel defense. See Rodgers v. Sargent Controls & Aerospace, 136 Cal. App. 4th 82, 88-89 (2006) ("[c]ollateral estoppel as a defense need not be alleged in an answer or other responsive pleading"). In any event, the First Amended Answer refers to a prior federal antitrust action (e.g., 5, 20, 24), and those facts are sufficient to support the defense. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DIANE LINEHAN VS. BAXTER INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Access To Decedent'S Electronic Medical Record At The Depositions Of St. Mary'S Medical Center Nurses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, May 10, 2013, Line 8, PLAINTIFF DIANE LINEHAN's Motion To Compel Access To Decedent'S Electronic Medical Record At The Depositions Of St. Mary'S Medical Center Nurses. Granted, for good cause shown. Decedent's electronic medical record (EMR) shall be produced at the depositions of the St. Mary's Medical Center nurses. Plaintiffs have shown that, in the circumstances of this case, discovery of the EMR at the deposition is necessary to fully and fairly discover decedent's complete medical record. The motion was timely filed. The parties shall meet and meet and confer in advance of the depositions to work out any logistical issues or potential problems regarding the actual taking of the video depositions, including workable physical arrangements for the room in which the depositions will be taken. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
